---
title: "RQ3 — Damage relative to area of control"
---

::: {.callout-important}
**Descriptive only — no comparison is made (DEC-005).** This is a spatial overlay of
2026 cropland damage against *indicative* government-controlled and former-AANES
areas. Boundaries are contested and treated as indicative only; the Euphrates split
of Deir ez-Zor is a **schematic proxy line**, not an authoritative control map.
**No differential or causal claim is made or implied** — this layer maps *where*
damage falls, and nothing about either administration faring better or worse
(`docs/PRODUCT.md` §5, §9).
:::

**Question (`docs/PRODUCT.md` §5).** Descriptively, where does the validated 2026
cropland damage fall relative to indicative areas of control?

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false
import sys, pathlib
import pandas as pd

REPO_ROOT = None
for _cand in (pathlib.Path.cwd(), *pathlib.Path.cwd().parents):
    if (_cand / "CLAUDE.md").exists() and (_cand / "viz").is_dir():
        REPO_ROOT = _cand
        sys.path.insert(0, str(_cand))
        break
assert REPO_ROOT is not None, "could not locate repo root (CLAUDE.md + viz/)"
RQ3 = REPO_ROOT / "outputs" / "analysis" / "rq3_control_overlay"
```

## What this is — and is not

- **Is:** a descriptive tabulation of *where* validated 2026 cropland damage falls
  relative to indicative control zones, at the shared schema's per-AOI granularity.
- **Is not:** a comparison. Nothing here states or implies that damage was
  higher/lower, or response better/worse, under either indicative administration.

A deliberate design choice: this page presents the tabulation as a **table, not a
side-by-side bar chart**, because a comparative chart would visually invite exactly
the differential reading DEC-005 forbids. Two structural facts make a comparison
impossible even if one were wanted: **Deir ez-Zor spans both indicative zones** and
the per-AOI schema cannot apportion its hectares between them (an RQ analysis may not
read the underlying rasters to do so — cross-reference discipline); and the national
fire-only governorates have **no drawn control geometry** at all.

## Descriptive tabulation — validated damaged cropland by indicative zone

```{python}
#| label: tbl-zones
#| echo: false
zones = pd.read_csv(RQ3 / "damage_by_indicative_zone.csv")
view = zones.rename(columns={
    "indicative_zone": "indicative zone", "aois": "AOIs",
    "flood_ha_headline_union": "flood ha", "fire_ha_headline_union": "fire ha",
    "total_ha_headline_union": "total ha",
})
view.style.hide(axis="index").format({
    "flood ha": "{:,.0f}", "fire ha": "{:,.0f}", "total ha": "{:,.0f}",
})
```

*Hectares are the union-cropland headline (DEC-015/032); flood figures are the peak
single event-date extent (no cross-date double-count, DEC-033). Listing side by side
is descriptive co-location, **not** a comparison.*

- **Deir ez-Zor** is reported as **spanning both** former-AANES (NE) and government
  (SW of the schematic Euphrates line), **unapportioned** — deliberately, not as an
  omission.
- The **national fire-only governorates** (Aleppo, Daraa, Hama, Homs, Idlib,
  Quneitra, Rural Damascus, Suwayda, Tartus) fall **outside** the drawn indicative
  control geometry; their burned cropland is reported for transparency with **no
  zone attributed**. Extending contested boundaries to force an overlay there would
  itself breach DEC-005.

## Caveats (every artifact carries these)

- INDICATIVE / CONTESTED boundaries — descriptive overlay only (DEC-005). NOT an
  authoritative control map. No differential or causal claim may be drawn. The
  Euphrates is a schematic proxy line; control geography is the indicative 2017–2024
  predominant pattern.
- Damaged hectares inherit the pipeline caveats: flood extent is open-water riverine
  inundation (DEC-023); burned cropland is VIIRS-confirmed dNBR, a conservative
  lower bound (DEC-031).
- **First-half-2026 scope (DEC-039):** figures are lower bounds; the summer
  harvest/fire peak is unobserved.
